examples: Her slate-blue skirts were kilted boldly about her waist and dovetailed behind her.
This was reputedly at the behest of an Englishman running an ironworks at Invergarry who felt his kilted employees needed a greater freedom of movement to do their work.
Before devolution, the impression was that the English did not really notice Scotland, regarding it at best as a kilted extension of the Lake District.
But then hundreds of Proposition 8's defenders turned up, accompanied by a band of kilted pipers, and there were arguments between the antagonists, our correspondent reports.
